headline: Agentic coding workflows consolidated around self-improving loops and Claude Code architecture while Claude Fable was discontinued.
body: The quarter's rotation centered on technique-level coding infrastructure: self-improvement, scoped skills, subagents, token optimization, and system architecture all moved together as developers built structured Claude Code environments with memory and observability [4][6]. Recursive self-improvement broke out alongside RAG, test-time behavior, and project management, visible in autonomous agent loops like Codex's Ralph and self-improving knowledge bases built on Karpathy's LLM wiki principles [7][9]. On the model side, OpenRouter's Fusion API drew scrutiny for claiming Fable-level performance through ensemble routing and a judge model, with testing showing it underperforms strong single models on practical coding and math tasks [1][10]. The window also marked Claude Fable's discontinuation against a wave of open-source releases including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, and MiniMax M3 [11].
